name: Coq Au Vin Telegraph
recipeInstructions:
A cast-iron casserole dish is ideal for this recipe. Alternatively, use a heavy frying pan, transferring to a casserole dish at the appropriate time.
1. Make the stock: In a large, heavy pan, add about 2 tablespoons of olive oil. Fry the chicken carcass and stock vegetables until they take on a good color.
2. Once everything is golden brown, pour in about 1 liter (2 pints) of water and bring to a boil. Simmer for 30-40 minutes, then strain and discard the solids.
3. Reduce the stock by rapid boiling to give approximately 250 ml (1/2 pint). Allow it to cool.
4. Cut the chicken into eight pieces and arrange in a shallow dish. Prepare the marinade vegetables: Mix the cooled wine and stock together, add the marinade vegetables, and pour over the chicken. Cover and leave overnight.
5. The following day, lift the chicken pieces from the marinade and pat dry. Cut the bacon into lardons.
6. In a heavy-lidded casserole dish, melt butter with 2 tablespoons of olive oil and cook half the lardons until the fat runs.
7. Fry the chicken pieces, a few at a time, until they are golden brown on all sides. Take your time as this frying gives the dish a more intense flavor.
8. When all the chicken is browned, return all the pieces to the casserole dish. Pour the marinade and vegetables over it and bring everything to a boil. Cover with a tight-fitting lid.
9. Simmer over a low heat for 90 minutes or place the covered dish in a preheated oven (160°C/325°F/Gas Mark 3) for the same length of time.
10. Meanwhile, peel the small onions and cook the remaining lardons in a heavy frying pan until the fat runs, then add the onions and cook until they begin to color.
11. Wipe the mushrooms clean.
12. Once the chicken is tender, remove the pieces from the sauce and keep warm. Strain the sauce, pressing hard on the vegetables.
13. Pour back the strained marinade into the casserole dish, then add the lardons, onions, and mushrooms. Simmer for 5 minutes.
14. Using a palate knife, knead together the butter and flour until you have a smooth paste. Drop pieces of this into the sauce, whisking gently until the sauce thickens.
15. Taste and correct the seasoning. Replace the chicken and simmer for an additional 3 to 4 minutes.
16. Cut the bread into croutons and fry in olive oil until golden. Rub with garlic and sprinkle with chopped parsley.
17. Serve the chicken with croutons and creamy mashed potatoes.
